name: Guillena Water Route Trail
name_original: Sendero Ruta del Agua de Guillena y Embalse Gergal — sendero por la Rivera de Huelva
description: Hike the Water Route of Guillena and Gergal Reservoir, a moderate 8.5-mile trail along the Rivera de Huelva with scenic views and history.
region_intro: Hiking around Guillena offers diverse landscapes, characterized by Mediterranean forests, scrubland, and the tranquil Rivera de Huelva. The region features accessible natural areas, including the prominent Gergal Reservoir and the Parque Periurbano El Gergal. Trails vary from gentle paths suitable for families to more challenging routes with moderate elevation changes, providing options for different fitness levels. Best hiking trails around Guillena The most popular hiking route is Guillena Water Route Trail, an 8.5 miles (13.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 43 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows the Rivera de Huelva, offering scenic views. Another top favourite among local hikers is Guillena Water Route Trail, a difficult 13.2 miles (21.3 km) path. This longer route circles the Gergal Reservoir, showcasing expansive Mediterranean landscapes. Local hikers also love the The Pines — Gergal Reservoir loop from Parque periurbano El Gergal, a 3.7 miles (6.0 km) trail leading through pleasant natural environments, often completed in about 1 hour 37 minutes. Hiking around Guillena is defined by Mediterranean landscapes, the Rivera de Huelva, and the Gergal Reservoir, with trails ranging from easy strolls to more challenging routes. The network offers options for different ability levels, including family-friendly hikes. The routes in Guillena are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.9 stars from over 80 reviews. More than 400 hikers have used komoot to explore Guillena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Guillena?
A: Guillena offers a diverse network of over 18 hiking trails, catering to various preferences and fitness levels. These routes explore the region's Mediterranean landscapes, the tranquil Rivera de Huelva, and the scenic Gergal Reservoir.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Guillena?
A: Yes, Guillena has several easy trails perfect for beginners and families. For instance, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14621286" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Pines — Gergal Reservoir loop from Parque periurbano El Gergal&lt;/a&gt; is a 6 km route that can be completed in about 1 hour 37 minutes, offering a pleasant natural environment without significant elevation changes. Another easy option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14621320" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the River at Sunset – Guillena Town Hall loop from Guillena&lt;/a&gt;, which is around 7.2 km.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes around Guillena?
A: Yes, circular routes are a popular feature in Guillena. A notable example is the more challenging 13.2-mile (21.3 km) &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010369577/guillena-water-route-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guillena Water Route Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which circles the Gergal Reservoir, showcasing expansive Mediterranean landscapes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14621286" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Pines — Gergal Reservoir loop from Parque periurbano El Gergal&lt;/a&gt; is also a great circular option for an easier experience.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Guillena?
A: Hikers in Guillena will experience typical Mediterranean landscapes, characterized by a mix of forests, scrubland, and open spaces. The trails often follow the Rivera de Huelva, offering tranquil river views, and many routes feature the scenic Gergal Reservoir, providing picturesque water vistas.

Q: What are the main natural attractions or landmarks along the hiking routes?
A: The primary natural attractions in Guillena are the Rivera de Huelva and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2703683" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gergal Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, which are central to many hiking experiences. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2694489" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta del Agua of the Gergal Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is a highlight in itself. While hiking, you might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2616148" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alamillo Park&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2710424" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Minilla Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; in the wider area.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Guillena?
A: The best time to hike in Guillena is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) months. During these seasons, the weather is milder and more comfortable for outdoor activities, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter conditions of winter.

Q: Are there options for longer, more challenging hikes in Guillena?
A: Yes, for those seeking a more challenging experience, Guillena offers longer routes. The difficult 13.2-mile (21.3 km) &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010369577/guillena-water-route-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guillena Water Route Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which circles the Gergal Reservoir, is a good option, taking approximately 5 hours and 36 minutes to complete.

Q: Is public transport available to access hiking trails in Guillena?
A: While Guillena is accessible by public transport, direct access to the starting points of all hiking trails might require some planning. It's advisable to check local bus schedules to Guillena town and then consider walking or local taxi services to reach specific trailheads, especially those further from the town center.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in Guillena?
A: Many popular trailheads in Guillena, especially those near the Parque Periurbano El Gergal or the starting points for the Water Route, offer designated parking areas. It's generally recommended to arrive early, especially on weekends, to secure a spot. Specific parking information can often be found on individual route descriptions.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Guillena?
A: The hiking trails in Guillena are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.9 stars from over 80 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the tranquility of the Rivera de Huelva, and the scenic beauty of the Gergal Reservoir. Many appreciate the variety of routes available for different skill levels.

Q: Are there any trails that feature water bodies or reservoirs?
A: Absolutely. Water features are a defining characteristic of hiking in Guillena. The prominent &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1012669181/guillena-water-route-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guillena Water Route Trail&lt;/a&gt; follows the Rivera de Huelva and circles the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2703683" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gergal Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, offering continuous scenic views of these water bodies.

Q: What is the typical terrain like on Guillena's hiking trails?
A: The terrain on Guillena's hiking trails is varied, reflecting its Mediterranean landscape. You can expect a mix of well-trodden paths, dirt tracks, and some rocky sections, especially on more challenging routes. Trails often pass through forests, scrubland, and open areas, with some gentle inclines and declines.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Water Route of Guillena and Gergal Reservoir?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas aren't detailed, the route is promoted by the Guillena Town Council, suggesting access points are likely available near Guillena. Many hikers start near the Gergal Reservoir or from Guillena itself. It's advisable to check local maps or community forums for the most convenient starting points and parking options.

faqs.question: Is the Water Route of Guillena and Gergal Reservoir dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially given the diverse wildlife and other hikers you may encounter. Please remember to clean up after your pet to maintain the natural beauty of the area.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The route can be enjoyed year-round, but spring and autumn are often ideal due to milder temperatures and vibrant natural colors. Summers can be hot, so an early start is recommended if hiking during those months. Some areas, particularly near the Gergal forest, can be humid, so consider this during wetter seasons.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to access the Water Route of Guillena and Gergal Reservoir. It is a publicly accessible trail promoted by the local council for ecological and sustainable development.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied but generally accessible. You'll mostly follow the courses of the Rivera de Huelva and River Cala, with gentle slopes and a sinuous path. Expect dirt roads, and sections that integrate an old mining railway line, featuring trenches, small bridges, and short tunnels. The riverbed can be alluvial with abundant stones in places.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: The full 13.7 km route is considered moderate, requiring good fitness. However, many sections are easily accessible, and shorter portions, such as an 8.5 km route with 140 meters of elevation gain, are described as very simple and ideal for beginners and families. You can choose a section that matches your group's skill level.

faqs.question: What notable sights or landmarks can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers a blend of natural beauty and historical interest. You'll encounter the beautiful Ruta del Agua Trail itself, enjoy views from the Mirador de la Solana Recio, and see the impressive Gergal Reservoir Dam. Other features include old railway stations, various reservoirs, quaint villages, and even the 19th-century Parladé Palace.

faqs.question: Are there rest areas or facilities along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is well-equipped with sixteen rest areas. These include shelters, viewing points, picnic areas, tables, benches, and litter bins, providing ample opportunities to pause, relax, and enjoy the surroundings. Informative signs are also present to enhance your experience.

faqs.question: What kind of flora and fauna might I encounter?
faqs.answer: The trail passes through a diverse Mediterranean forest environment. You'll see holm oaks, gall oaks, pines, poplars, and cork oaks, along with scrubland featuring palm hearts and lavender. The area is rich in indigenous flora and fauna, creating a vibrant 'nature museum' with diverse colors, sounds, and smells.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route overlaps with several other paths. Notably, it covers significant portions of the Ruta del Agua (94%), Ruta del Agua de Guillena (82%), and Sendero Ruta del Agua de Guillena (82%). It also intersects with sections of the Sendero del Ferrocarril (32%) and the Antigua linea ferrea minas de Cala - San Juan de Aznalfarache.

faqs.question: Are there opportunities for other outdoor activities nearby?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! Beyond hiking, the area around the Water Route offers a wide range of complementary tourist attractions and outdoor activities. These include swimming, fishing, cycling, and horse riding, making it a great destination for a full day or weekend of outdoor adventure.
